title: Treehouse Of Horror Xxii

jokes.text: Your mother is the switch witch... a sort of tooth fairy dealie. I take your sugary sweets and I give you healthy items. Plain brown toothbrushes, flavored dental floss and fun-sized mouthwashes... TSA approved.
jokes.setup: Kids ask who the switch witch is and why she wants their candy
jokes.punchline: Mundane health items described as Halloween treats, capped with TSA approval detail
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion of Halloween candy exchange with health items, TSA detail adds contemporary absurdist touch

jokes.text: and fun-sized mouthwashes... TSA approved.
jokes.setup: Switch witch giving healthy alternatives to candy
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific detail about TSA approval for tiny mouthwash
jokes.explanation: Clever contemporary reference mixing Halloween candy with post-9/11 travel restrictions. Good specificity and absurdist detail.

jokes.text: This is exactly why kids need a union.
jokes.setup: Adult imposing unwanted trade of candy for health items
jokes.punchline: Child responds with adult labor relations concept
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart response - applying adult concepts to childhood grievances with great economy

jokes.text: The switch witch is me. You know, on some level, I've always known.
jokes.setup: Homer claims he'd never eat the candy because he's scared of the switch witch
jokes.punchline: Marge reveals she IS the switch witch, Homer's nonsensical acceptance
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer obliviousness followed by fake profundity - very character-specific

jokes.text: I've got candy, skip to my Lou / My insulin will spike, that's what it's gonna do / I'm gonna buy me some diabetic shoes / Skip to my Lou my...
jokes.setup: Traditional cheerful children's song 'Skip to My Lou'
jokes.punchline: Lyrics about diabetes complications maintaining the tune
jokes.explanation: Dark health consequences set to innocent children's tune - good contrast and Homer characterization

jokes.text: Hello, 911? I need a helicopter rescue and some cold milk.
jokes.setup: Homer trapped on telephone pole after candy mishap
jokes.punchline: Treats minor candy emergency as major rescue operation, requests milk
jokes.explanation: Homer's dramatic overreaction plus mundane milk request shows his skewed priorities

jokes.text: The only sane thing to do is chew off my arm.
jokes.setup: Homer can't wait 20 minutes for helicopter rescue
jokes.punchline: Self-amputation presented as reasonable solution for candy access
jokes.explanation: Extreme solution for minor problem delivered as logical conclusion - peak Homer logic

jokes.text: I'm not gonna swallow, 'cause I want to save room for candy.
jokes.setup: Homer chewing off his own arm
jokes.punchline: Shows restraint to save appetite for candy while eating himself
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Homer logic - dietary restraint while committing cannibalism for candy access

jokes.text: I'm really getting the hang of this.
jokes.setup: Homer has been eating his own arm
jokes.punchline: Treats self-amputation as learnable skill he's improving at
jokes.explanation: Casual pride in horrific self-harm for candy - captures Homer's oblivious optimism

jokes.text: Can his funeral be on a school day?
jokes.setup: Family learning Homer is paralyzed, expressing love and hope
jokes.punchline: Bart immediately thinks of practical school absence benefit
jokes.explanation: Perfectly callous Bart response to tragedy - great economy and character consistency

jokes.text: This is so horrible. I can't speak!
jokes.setup: Lisa expressing horror at Homer's condition
jokes.punchline: Claims she can't speak while actively speaking
jokes.explanation: Basic irony joke - functional but predictable contradiction

jokes.text: So far, a normal Sunday morning.
jokes.setup: Homer describing being paralyzed and unable to move on the floor
jokes.punchline: Treats catastrophic injury as typical Sunday routine
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer minimization of serious situation - good character-specific understatement

jokes.text: Ah, Halloween. The one time of year when the squalor of our home works to our advantage.
jokes.setup: Homer reminiscing about decorating for Halloween
jokes.punchline: Admits their home is squalid but useful for spooky holiday atmosphere
jokes.explanation: Self-aware humor about the Simpson home's condition with good vocabulary choice ('squalor')

jokes.text: A real spider would get so mad if I did this. I wonder if this thing has batteries.
jokes.setup: Homer squeezing what he thinks is a fake Halloween spider
jokes.punchline: Anthropomorphizes real spider emotions, then wonders about batteries
jokes.explanation: Homer's childlike reasoning about spider feelings and fake/real confusion sets up the bite

jokes.text: Hmm. Well, at least I still look good. Okay, handsome, that's enough primping.
jokes.setup: Homer just got bitten by a spider and is paralyzed
jokes.punchline: Immediately focuses on his appearance and talks to himself admiringly
jokes.explanation: Homer's vanity and delusion about his looks perfectly timed after catastrophe

jokes.text: Ooh, cleavage.
jokes.setup: Marge giving heartfelt speech about pure, patient, supportive love
jokes.punchline: Homer gets distracted by Marge's cleavage mid-speech
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer distraction from serious moment - great economy but predictable

jokes.text: Oh, my God. She's still on the first sentence! Must make her stop.
jokes.setup: Lisa reading The Brothers Karamazov's notoriously long opening sentence
jokes.punchline: Homer's internal panic about Lisa's verbose reading choice
jokes.explanation: Good literary reference humor - anyone who's read Dostoevsky knows that opening sentence is endless

jokes.text: Can you pass gas at will? Fart once for no, twice for yes.
jokes.setup: Family discovers Homer can communicate by farting accidentally
jokes.punchline: Bart immediately systematizes crude communication method
jokes.explanation: Bart's practical approach to crude bodily function communication system - very character appropriate

jokes.text: D, E, A... Dearest Marge, though my body cannot move, my heart still beats and my brain still brains.
jokes.setup: Homer spelling out a heartfelt letter to Marge through fart-alphabet
jokes.punchline: 'My brain still brains' - verb usage of 'brain' instead of 'thinks'
jokes.explanation: Homer's attempt at eloquence produces malapropism 'brains' as verb - very characteristic

jokes.text: Although if it requires months of difficult physical therapy, I'll pass.
jokes.setup: Homer expressing hope for a cure in his heartfelt letter
jokes.punchline: Immediately dismisses cure if it involves any actual effort
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment - wants the benefit but none of the work, undercuts romantic letter

jokes.text: more than my butt can say
jokes.setup: Homer dictating love letter about missing holding Marge
jokes.punchline: References his new butt-based communication method
jokes.explanation: Clever callback that works the established communication gag into romantic context. Good wordplay on 'say.'

jokes.text: He's got the proportionate strength of a paralyzed spider!
jokes.setup: Homer gets spider powers from second spider bite
jokes.punchline: Parodies Spider-Man catchphrase but with useless paralyzed spider strength
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Spider-Man parody that perfectly subverts the expected power upgrade with useless abilities

jokes.text: Forget paying for my kid's operation... I'm out of here!
jokes.setup: Doctor witnessing Homer's bizarre spider powers
jokes.punchline: Abandons medical ethics and patient care to flee supernatural situation
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about medical professional abandoning duties, adds absurdist detail about kid's operation

jokes.text: They say no two ass-webs are the same. Beautiful in its way.
jokes.setup: Homer shooting webs from inappropriate body part
jokes.punchline: Marge applies snowflake uniqueness wisdom to crude bodily function
jokes.explanation: Marge's attempt to find beauty in Homer's crude spider powers - great contrast of highbrow sentiment with lowbrow reality

jokes.text: Springfield, my hometown. Pretty little place. Although, even the Garden of Eden could use a nice cleansing rain now and then.
jokes.setup: Ned admiring Springfield in peaceful, wholesome narration
jokes.punchline: Biblical reference masks his plan for mass murder as 'cleansing rain'
jokes.explanation: Sinister undertone delivered in Ned's wholesome tone - biblical language concealing murder plans

jokes.text: Well, those who can't do, teach.
jokes.setup: Patty/Selma failing at seduction
jokes.punchline: Applies common saying about teaching to their failed pickup attempt
jokes.explanation: Decent application of familiar saying to new context - functional but not exceptional

jokes.text: Slay Montgomery Burns and pee in his ashes!
jokes.setup: God giving Ned divine murder instructions
jokes.punchline: Sacred divine command includes juvenile bathroom humor
jokes.explanation: Juxtaposition of divine commandment with crude desecration - shock humor with biblical parody

jokes.text: If you're having trouble with the second part, drink a lot of water. Now I've got to go. A hip-hop star is thanking me at the VMAs.
jokes.setup: God instructing Ned to urinate on Burns' ashes
jokes.punchline: Practical urination advice followed by contemporary award show appearance
jokes.explanation: God's casual practical advice plus modern celebrity culture reference - good absurdist escalation

jokes.text: It's like a chigga-chigga-chigga-chigga. But you only hear it when you're driving over 30. And you never hear it when you take it to the shop. Then it sounds perfect.
jokes.setup: God asking Ned to diagnose car trouble as divine mission
jokes.punchline: Omnipotent deity describes universal car repair frustration
jokes.explanation: Perfect relatable car trouble observation from all-powerful deity - great mundane/divine contrast

jokes.text: Come on! God does crazy things! Check your Old Testament.
jokes.setup: Ned questioning if God really wants him to kill ice cream man
jokes.punchline: Bart defending divine murder commands with biblical precedent
jokes.explanation: Smart biblical reference with Bart's casual tone - good economy and religious knowledge

jokes.text: Hell! Damn! Backside!
jokes.setup: Ned declaring nothing matters anymore since he's going to Hell
jokes.punchline: His idea of extreme profanity includes 'backside'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned character moment - even in moral rebellion, can't truly curse, 'backside' is hilariously tame

jokes.text: There is no Hell, and there is no God. If there were, would He let me do this?
jokes.setup: Homer asserting atheism to deranged Ned
jokes.punchline: Immediately gets strangled, disproving his point through physical comedy
jokes.explanation: Classic setup/immediate contradiction through physical violence - good ironic structure

jokes.text: Get me a coffee!
jokes.setup: Satan revealed as Homer's new boss in Hell
jokes.punchline: Ultimate evil being makes mundane office demands
jokes.explanation: Standard hell-as-office joke - functional but not original, good economy

jokes.text: Could this get any worse?
jokes.setup: God surveying the chaos he's created
jokes.punchline: Sets up visual reveal of Satan as his boss downstairs
jokes.explanation: Classic setup line that works to establish the visual gag. The theological hierarchy joke is solid but familiar territory.

jokes.text: Are we in Kansas anymore? No. Well, are we in Nebraska? No! We are not in any state! Oh, oh, is it Michigan?
jokes.setup: Krusty explaining they're on alien planet
jokes.punchline: Ralph's persistent geographical guessing including Michigan as worst option
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ralph obliviousness with Michigan as punchline state - good character consistency

jokes.text: Gotta play a Nazi party rally. Oh, yeah, they're back.
jokes.setup: Krusty explaining why he needs hilarity gas
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions Nazis are back, treats as routine booking
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Nazi resurgence treated as mundane entertainment booking - good deadpan delivery

jokes.text: Whoa! Check out this bitchin' bod!
jokes.setup: Bart controlling his alien avatar body
jokes.punchline: Impressed by his own alien physique using dated slang
jokes.explanation: Bart's enthusiasm with dated slang - character appropriate but not particularly clever

jokes.text: These avatars cost $80 billion each. But your human body costs nothing.
jokes.setup: Bullies explaining why they can't beat up Bart's avatar
jokes.punchline: Human life valued at zero compared to expensive alien technology
jokes.explanation: Dark economic commentary on human vs. technology value - good social satire

jokes.text: That's a Japanese outlet, Bart. You need an adapter.
jokes.setup: Bart trying to connect to alien technology
jokes.punchline: Lisa treating alien planet tech like international travel problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa practical knowledge applied to completely wrong situation - great character moment

jokes.text: Where you go? Where you go? No meter, is cheaper, yeah? Suit yourself, you one-eyed bastard.
jokes.setup: Alien taxi offering ride to Homer
jokes.punchline: Universal taxi driver experience with alien insult
jokes.explanation: Universal taxi driver behavior transcends species - good observational humor with alien twist

jokes.text: You don't have to yell! I am not yelling! This is my seductive voice!
jokes.setup: Bart commenting on Kamala's volume
jokes.punchline: Alien considers loud aggressive speech seductive
jokes.explanation: Cultural miscommunication joke about seduction styles - functional character moment

jokes.text: Now you've murdered our brothers, the flowers! Stupid! Stupid! Now you're having sex with Uncle Tree!
jokes.setup: Kamala explaining that everything on the planet is alive and related
jokes.punchline: Bart's innocent actions become murder and inappropriate relations
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of environmental morality taken to absurd extreme - murder to inappropriate relations

jokes.text: And now let us touch testicles and mate for life. Don't you mean tentacles? I know what I said!
jokes.setup: Alien courtship ritual proposal
jokes.punchline: Bart's innocent correction reveals inappropriate anatomy reference
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke with anatomy wordplay - Bart's innocent correction gets shut down

jokes.text: It was serviceable!
jokes.setup: Bart asking how their mating experience was
jokes.punchline: Damning with faint praise using business-like terminology
jokes.explanation: Perfect underwhelming review of intimate experience - great economy and alien directness

jokes.text: It's a place on the other side of this planet that's very rainy so you can only see one moon. You know, like Portland.
jokes.setup: Bart caught mentioning Earth, trying to cover
jokes.punchline: Explains Earth as local region, Portland as rainy reference point
jokes.explanation: Good quick thinking cover story with Portland rain stereotype - clever geographic misdirection

jokes.text: But on Rigel Seven, lack of eye contact and too many details indicate the telling of truth.
jokes.setup: Bart giving suspicious over-detailed explanation
jokes.punchline: Alien culture interprets human lying behavior as truthfulness
jokes.explanation: Clever cultural inversion of human body language interpretation - original anthropological humor

jokes.text: Feel the wonder of one million fetuses!
jokes.setup: Announcing pregnancy to proud father
jokes.punchline: Extreme alien reproduction numbers presented enthusiastically
jokes.explanation: Shock humor with alien reproduction biology - moderately effective gross-out comedy

jokes.text: That only keeps me from giving birth while we are having sex.
jokes.setup: Bart asking about birth control after learning about pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Alien birth control prevents mid-intercourse birth, not conception
jokes.explanation: Absurd alien reproduction logic - functional gross-out humor with creative biology

jokes.text: Unfortunately our environment is too hostile to grow pickles. And the only flavor ice cream we have is butter brickle. To repeat: no pickles, butter brickle.
jokes.setup: Explaining pregnancy cravings will be hard to satisfy
jokes.punchline: Terrible ice cream flavor is only option, repeats for emphasis
jokes.explanation: Good pregnancy craving humor with universally unpopular ice cream flavor - relatable situation made worse

jokes.text: Let's fry these calamari and dip 'em in a sauce made of their own blood! And wine from their own grapes. That's not so bad. It's a little bad.
jokes.setup: Planning to attack squid-like aliens
jokes.punchline: Cannibalistic cooking plan followed by wine discussion deflating the threat
jokes.explanation: Dark military humor deflated by mundane wine discussion - good tension break

jokes.text: We have no tanks. Our planet will protect us. Oh, man, you sound like my art teacher. Look a little like her, too.
jokes.setup: Kamala expressing faith in planetary defense
jokes.punchline: Bart compares environmental philosophy to his art teacher
jokes.explanation: Bart connecting alien environmentalism to school experience - good character-specific observation

jokes.text: And I got them from Milhouse.
jokes.setup: Kamala revealing she has space warts
jokes.punchline: Bart traces STD back to Milhouse
jokes.explanation: Crude humor connecting alien STD to Milhouse - economy is good but fairly predictable

jokes.text: Yo, Colonel! Uh, you've got some schmutz on your cheek. Where? Here? Skinner...!
jokes.setup: Bart helpfully pointing out dirt on Skinner's face
jokes.punchline: Guides Skinner into getting killed by mechanical equipment
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection leading to Skinner's demise - Bart's helpful tone makes it funnier

jokes.text: Had they asked for the Hilarium, we would simply have given it to them. In Rigelian there is no word for 'yours' or 'mine.' That's the reason we didn't enjoy the movie Yours, Mine and Ours.
jokes.setup: Explaining their sharing culture after destructive invasion
jokes.punchline: Cultural linguistic difference ruined specific movie experience
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from philosophical sharing culture to specific movie review - unexpected and clever

jokes.text: Knitting one sweater for someone costs 27 Americans their jobs.
jokes.setup: Warning against making homemade Christmas gifts
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific economic calculation for craft activity
jokes.explanation: Sharp economic satire with absurdly precise statistics - good commentary on consumerism

jokes.text: Christmas is a wonderful time to take up alcoholism. Come on, you see your family all year round. The holidays are for your bartender.
jokes.setup: Holiday advice about Christmas spirit
jokes.punchline: Promotes alcoholism as family coping mechanism, prioritizes bartender
jokes.explanation: Dark holiday humor about family stress and alcohol dependency - cynical but relatable

tmdb_overview: A boulder traps Homer's arm when he takes a drive through a canyon; a spider bite paralyzes Homer; Flanders becomes a vigilante by night; Bart and Milhouse travel to a distant planet to obtain an extract.
